spec_match.rationale: The demo applies the exact path-length normalization contract to SVG path dash calculations and distinguishes the CSS property from its attribute fallback.

interactive.rationale: Shape, progress, and total controls update the path and readouts. Progress is now modeled as a true 0-100% fraction of the selected normalized total, so drawn units stay within [0, total] and the dash pattern is always valid.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature blurb promises normalized dash-driven progress without geometric measurement, and this concept provides that interaction with an honest pre-150 fallback.

edge_cases.rationale: The former total=100/progress=max stale-render bug is fixed: at that edge the readout reports 100% drawn and stroke-dasharray resolves to '100 0' (a non-negative gap that CSS accepts) instead of the previous '1000 -900'. Verified in headless Chromium 141 by loading the concept with edge-case default values and reading the computed stroke-dasharray.

references.rationale: The page links directly to ChromeStatus feature 4861677550043136 and the SVG specification pull request.

conformance.rationale: A feature-level conformance suite now exists (v150/support-path-length-as-a-css-property/conformance.json) asserting path-length CSS.supports, the zero edge case, and a CSSOM round-trip; verified to execute in headless Chromium (fails honestly on this pre-150 build).

runtime_evidence.rationale: Verified in headless Chromium 141 via Bash (no chrome-devtools-mcp in the weekly cloud routine): render() runs on load, the default and edge-case dash values were read from the DOM, and the conformance route executed. Full DevTools accessibility-tree and click-through evidence still pending a local MCP session.

telemetry_health.rationale: The shared telemetry client loads and POSTs successfully, but individual shape and range interactions are not exposed as inspectable interaction events.

design_system.rationale: The page uses shared color variables, provides an interactive stage rather than a static card, and has no local button color override risk.

accessibility.rationale: Native select and range controls have accessible names and keyboard focus, but visible labels are not programmatically associated and changing progress/readout state has no live-region semantics.

openQuestions.title: Associate visible labels and announce live output
openQuestions.detail: Use for/id label associations instead of relying only on aria-label, and expose the changing percentage/code/readouts through an appropriate status or live region without making slider movement excessively verbose.

summary: Weekly hardening pass (headless Chromium 141 via Bash, no chrome-devtools-mcp). Fixed the major edge-case bug: progress is now derived as a fraction of the selected normalized total, so drawn units are clamped to [0, total] and the dash gap can never go negative. Verified by serving the concept with edge-case default values (progress=max, total=min) and reading the resulting DOM: readout '100% drawn', stroke-dasharray '100 0'. The feature-level conformance suite was also created this pass, resolving the missing-suite major question. Remaining: programmatic label association and a live region for the readouts (moderate).
